title: Age And Death.
content:
Come closer, kind, white, long-familiar friend,
        Embrace me, fold me to thy broad, soft breast.
    Life has grown strange and cold, but thou dost bend
        Mild eyes of blessing wooing to my rest.
    So often hast thou come, and from my side
    So many hast thou lured, I only bide
    Thy beck, to follow glad thy steps divine.
        Thy world is peopled for me; this world's bare.
        Through all these years my couch thou didst prepare.
    Thou art supreme Love - kiss me - I am thine!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emma Lazarus represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Emma Lazarus's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
